quiz.title: Unit 1 Quiz 4, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Properties, Compounds, and Phase Changes
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Physical Properties, Compounds vs Elements, Precipitate Formation, Exothermic Reactions, Phase Changes (Melting, Freezing, Evaporation, Condensation)

questions.question: Which property would be MOST useful for identifying an unknown substance?
questions.explanation: Density is an intensive physical property that remains constant regardless of sample size, making it useful for identification. Volume, mass, and shape are extensive properties that vary with sample size.

questions.question: Which change is typically reversible?
questions.explanation: Melting ice is a physical change that is reversible by freezing. Rusting, combustion, and decomposition are chemical changes that produce new substances and are not easily reversible.

questions.question: A student notes that a substance is odorless. This observation refers to a:
questions.explanation: Odor is a physical property—it can be observed without changing the substance's identity. It describes how the substance interacts with our sense of smell.

questions.question: Which pair of properties are BOTH physical?
questions.explanation: Melting point and color are both physical properties—observable without chemical change. Flammability, reactivity, toxicity, corrosion, and acidity are chemical properties.

questions.question: A student dissolves a white powder in water. Later, the water evaporates and the solid reappears unchanged. This observation BEST supports that:
questions.explanation: The powder reappearing unchanged demonstrates that dissolving was a physical change—no new substance formed. The original substance can be recovered by evaporation.

questions.question: Select all that apply: Which of the following is a compound?
questions.explanation: Water (H₂O), sodium chloride (NaCl), and carbon dioxide (CO₂) are compounds—chemically bonded elements in fixed ratios. Oxygen (O₂) and carbon (C) are elements.

questions.question: Select all compounds:
questions.explanation: Methane (CH₄) and glucose (C₆H₁₂O₆) are compounds containing multiple elements chemically bonded. Hydrogen, iron, and helium are elements.

questions.question: Select all that apply: Which of the following are compounds?
questions.explanation: Ammonia (NH₃), salt (NaCl), and sugar (C₁₂H₂₂O₁₁) are compounds with fixed ratios of different elements. Nitrogen is an element (diatomic), and neon is a noble gas element.

questions.question: NaCl is known as ____________
questions.explanation: NaCl is a chemical formula representing sodium chloride. Chemical symbols represent elements (Na, Cl), and equations show reactions.

questions.question: When two liquids mix and a solid forms, this is known as ______.
questions.explanation: A precipitate is a solid that forms when two solutions react chemically. This indicates a chemical change has occurred.

questions.question: The release of heat from a chemical reaction shows that it is ________.
questions.explanation: Exothermic reactions release heat energy to the surroundings. Endothermic reactions absorb heat.

questions.question: When a solid changes to a liquid due to heat gain, this is known as __________.
questions.explanation: Melting (fusion) is the phase change from solid to liquid upon heat gain. Freezing is the reverse (liquid to solid).

questions.question: When a liquid changes to a solid due to heat loss, this is known as __________.
questions.explanation: Freezing (solidification) is the phase change from liquid to solid upon heat loss.

questions.question: When a liquid changes to gas, this is known as ____________.
questions.explanation: Evaporation (vaporization) is the phase change from liquid to gas. Condensation is gas to liquid.

questions.question: When a gas changes to liquid, this is known as __________.
questions.explanation: Condensation is the phase change from gas to liquid, releasing heat energy.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered compounds, properties, and phase changes! 🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the difference between elements and compounds.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on phase change terminology and physical vs chemical properties.
scoreMessages.0: Please review intensive vs extensive properties, compound identification, and phase changes.
